records.ety: [OE. knelen, cneolien; akin to D. knielen, Dan. knæle. See Knee.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Knelt (nlt) or Kneeled (nēld); p. pr. & vb. n. Kneeling.]

defs: To bend the knee; to fall or rest on the knees; -- sometimes with down.
quotes: And he kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, Lord, lay not this sin to their charge.
As soon as you are dressed, kneel and say the Lord's Prayer.
notes: ☞ The act of kneeling, when performed in front of a person, is often done as a sign of respect, humility, or supplication. It has a similar significance when performed in front of religious objects, such as an altar or shrine.
